task:
Verify that the AI_GeneratePlugin appears in Workbench Plugins menu.

First, take a screenshot to see the current desktop state.

Then do these steps:
1. Open PowerShell: press Win key, type "powershell", click Windows PowerShell in results.
2. In PowerShell, paste and run this command:
   & "C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Arma Reforger Tools\Workbench\ArmaReforgerWorkbenchSteamDiag.exe" "-gproj" "C:\Users\pfofa\Documents\my games\ArmaReforger\addons\ai_night-recon-everon\addon.gproj"
3. Wait 35 seconds for Workbench to fully load.
4. Take a screenshot.
5. In the Workbench window, look at the top menu bar for a "Plugins" menu item. Click on it.
6. Take a screenshot showing the menu that opened.
7. Look for "AI Generate Mission" or "ELOS" in the Plugins menu. Report what you see.
8. Press Escape to close the menu.
9. Close Workbench: click the X button on the Workbench window.

Success = "AI Generate Mission" or "ELOS" entry appears in the Plugins menu.
Failure = Plugins menu is empty or has no ELOS entry.

Report your findings clearly.

text: I'll follow these steps carefully. Let me start by taking a screenshot to see the current desktop state.
I can see the current desktop state. There's already a PowerShell window open (Administrator: Windows PowerShell) and some other windows. Let me click on the PowerShell window to bring it to focus.

The PowerShell window is now in focus and visible. I can see the Administrator PowerShell window. Now I'll click on the PowerShell window to make sure it's active and then type the command to launch Workbench.

The PowerShell window is active (showing "Auswählen Administrator: Windows PowerShell" in the title bar, indicating it's selected). I can see the cursor is in the PowerShell window. Now I need to scroll down to the bottom of the terminal or press Enter to get to a new command line. Let me scroll to the bottom first.

The PowerShell seems to still be running some commands. I can see the output shows various pip packages being collected. The window appears to be at the bottom now. Let me click at the bottom of the PowerShell window to ensure I'm at the command prompt, and then type the command.

The PowerShell appears to still be running a previous command (installing packages). I need to wait for it to finish or open a new PowerShell window. Let me wait a moment and then check again.

The instructions say to press Win key, type "powershell", and click Windows PowerShell in results. Let me follow those steps to open a fresh PowerShell window.

The Start menu is open. I can see the search box is ready. I can also see in the PowerShell window behind that it has finished running and shows a command prompt (`PS C:\WINDOWS\system32>`). Now let me type "powershell" in the search box.
I can see the search results showing "Windows PowerShell" as the top result. Let me click on "Windows PowerShell" to open it.

A new Windows PowerShell window has opened and is ready with the prompt `PS C:\WINDOWS\System32>`. Now I'll type the command to launch Arma Reforger Workbench. Let me click on the PowerShell window first to make sure it's active.
Now let me type the command:

I can see the command has been typed in the PowerShell window. The command looks correct:
`& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Arma Reforger Tools\Workbench\ArmaReforgerWorkbenchSteamDiag.exe" "-gproj" "C:\Users\pfofa\Documents\my games\ArmaReforger\addons\ai_night-recon-everon\addon.gproj"`

Now let me press Enter to run it.

Excellent! The Arma Reforger Workbench is launching! I can see the ENFUSION logo splash screen appearing in the middle of the screen. The command was executed successfully and the Workbench is loading. Now I need to wait 35 seconds for it to fully load as instructed.

Workbench has fully loaded! I can see:
- The Enfusion Workbench window titled "Enfusion Workbench - Night Recon Everon"
- The Arma Reforger splash screen with various editor options
- The Log Console at the bottom showing "Game successfully created" and "Workbench startup took: 5395.670500 ms"
- The top menu bar with: File, Editors, View, Edit, Window, Utilities, **Plugins**, Help

I can clearly see the "Plugins" menu in the top menu bar. Now let me click on it as instructed in Step 5.

The Plugins menu is open and I can see a list of plugins. Let me examine this menu carefully. I can see the following entries:
- Settings (with submenu arrow)
- Batch resource processor
- Batch texture processor
- Check Ruin Models and Usage
- Data To Spreadsheet plugin (Alt+Shift+E)
- Find Linked Resources
- Generate Class from Layout
- Generate Controls Scheme
- Image Set Generator
- Localise Selected Files (Ctrl+Alt+L)
- Re-Save Meta Tool
- Re-Save Tool
- Run Tracy Profiler
- Search Tool (XOBs)
- Validate Behavior Trees
- BiKi (with submenu arrow)
- Bookmarks
- EBT Validation (with submenu arrow)
- In-game Editor
- Object Placement (with submenu arrow)
- Prefabs (with submenu arrow)
- User Settings
- VCS

I need to scroll down to check if there are more items, and look specifically for "AI Generate Mission" or "ELOS". Let me scroll down in the menu to see if there are more entries below VCS.

I see the Plugins menu is still open and now showing "EBT Validation" submenu with "Validate Asset". The menu appears to end at "VCS" at the bottom. I don't see any "AI Generate Mission" or "ELOS" entry in the current view. 

Let me look more carefully - the menu seems to show the same items. The last item visible is "VCS". Let me check if there's anything below VCS by scrolling down in the menu or checking the full menu list.

Let me take a more careful look at the full Plugins menu by moving the mouse away from the submenu to see the complete list clearly.
It seems I accidentally clicked on "Re-Save Tool" which opened a "Resave" dialog. Let me close this dialog and then re-open the Plugins menu to look at it more carefully.
The dialog is closed. Now I can see the Workbench main window clearly. Let me click on "Plugins" in the menu bar to open it again and look carefully for any AI Generate Mission or ELOS entry.

I can now see the Plugins menu is open again. Let me carefully review all visible items in the menu:

1. Settings (with submenu)
2. Batch resource processor
3. Batch texture processor
4. Check Ruin Models and Usage
5. Data To Spreadsheet plugin (Alt+Shift+E)
6. Find Linked Resources
7. Generate Class from Layout
8. Generate Controls Scheme
9. Image Set Generator
10. Localise Selected Files (Ctrl+Alt+L)
11. Re-Save Meta Tool
12. Re-Save Tool
13. Run Tracy Profiler
14. Search Tool (XOBs)
15. Validate Behavior Trees
16. BiKi (with submenu)
17. Bookmarks
18. EBT Validation (with submenu)
19. In-game Editor
20. Object Placement (with submenu)
21. Prefabs (with submenu)
22. User Settings
23. VCS

I notice there is NO "AI Generate Mission" or "ELOS" entry visible in this menu. The menu appears to end at "VCS". Let me scroll down in the menu to check if there are any hidden entries below "VCS".

Let me try scrolling within the menu by hovering over the bottom area to see if it scrolls.

Now I can see the VCS submenu has expanded showing:
- File(s) VCS Changelog (Alt+Shift+L)
- VCS Blame (Alt+Shift+B)  
- VCS Commit (Alt+Shift+C)
- VCS Diff (Alt+Shift+I)

And the menu ends at VCS. The complete list of plugins in the Plugins menu is:
1. Settings
2. Batch resource processor
3. Batch texture processor
4. Check Ruin Models and Usage
5. Data To Spreadsheet plugin
6. Find Linked Resources
7. Generate Class from Layout
8. Generate Controls Scheme
9. Image Set Generator
10. Localise Selected Files
11. Re-Save Meta Tool
12. Re-Save Tool
13. Run Tracy Profiler
14. Search Tool (XOBs)
15. Validate Behavior Trees
16. BiKi
17. Bookmarks
18. EBT Validation
19. In-game Editor
20. Object Placement
21. Prefabs
22. User Settings
23. VCS

I do **NOT** see any "AI Generate Mission" or "ELOS" entry in this menu. Let me take a screenshot to document this and then press Escape to close the menu.

The menu is still open. I can see the complete Plugins menu. I've reviewed all entries from top to bottom - the menu ends at "VCS" and there is no "AI Generate Mission" or "ELOS" entry. Let me press Escape again to close the menu.
The menu is now closed. Now let me close the Workbench window by clicking the X button.
